headlines.title: Special Report: Looking back on the political legacy of Sen. Lindsey Graham
headlines.description: Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who had held his Senate seat since 2003 and was seen as a war hawk and significant voice on defense and foreign policy, has died at the age of 71 from a “sudden illness.” NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin and Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker report on Graham’s rise in Congress, his relationship with President Donald Trump, and his views on the wars in Iran and between Russia and Ukraine.

headlines.title: Trump remembers his last call with Lindsey Graham the night before his death: Full interview
headlines.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Donald Trump reacts to the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.).

headlines.title: Netanyahu says he's lost a 'beloved friend' after the death of Lindsey Graham: Full interview
headlines.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu remembers the late Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) for his “clarity… courage… and convictions.”

headlines.title: Tim Scott remembers how Lindsey Graham ‘committed to the people he loved’: Full interview
headlines.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Sen. Tim Scott (R-S.C.) reacts to the death of his fellow South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham (R).

headlines.title: Adam Schiff remembers Lindsey Graham as ‘larger than life’: Full interview
headlines.description: Sen. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.) joins Meet the Press to reflect on the passing of his colleague Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) and discuss the U.S. war with Iran as both countries continue to trade strikes.

headlines.title: US Launches New Strikes on Iran After It Closes Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: The United States launched new attacks on Iran after the Iranians, again, shut down the Strait of Hormuz. This comes hours after Iran’s new supreme leader vowed revenge for the death of his father in the opening hours of the war. NBC’s Raf Sanchez reports for Sunday TODAY.

headlines.title: Iran’s supreme leader vows revenge for father’s killing
headlines.description: Iranian state media reports that the Revolutionary Guard says the Strait of Hormuz is closed until further notice. It comes as Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is vowing revenge for his father’s killing, saying it is the “demand” of his country. Khamenei has not been seen since he was severely injured when the war began. His father was killed early in the war by U.S. and Israeli air strikes. NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin has the latest.

headlines.title: England defeats Norway for World Cup semifinals spot
headlines.description: In extra time, England defeated Norway to clinch a spot in the World Cup semifinals. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch reports.

headlines.title: Argentina elimina a Suiza y así reaccionaron los protagonistas del partido
headlines.description: Lionel Scaloni destacó el esfuerzo de Argentina. Julián Álvarez, Lautaro Martínez, José Manuel López y Leandro Paredes compartieron sus sensaciones, mientras Gregor Kobel y Murat Yakin lamentaron la eliminación de Suiza.

headlines.title: Scaloni tras el pase a Semifinal ante Inglaterra: "Es un partido de fútbol, no hay más que eso"
headlines.description: Pese al enorme peso histórico del duelo de Argentina ante los ingleses y las Malvinas de por medio, el DT Campeón del Mundo apuntó solo a lo futbolístico; además, dio mérito a sus jugadores tras el triunfo ante Suiza, explicó el gol de Mac Allister y más.

headlines.title: Radar Mundial: Inglaterra y Argentina completan unas semifinales de lujo
headlines.description: Inglaterra eliminó a Noruega con un doblete de Jude Bellingham y Argentina venció a Suiza con goles de Mac Allister, Julián Álvarez y Lautaro Martínez. Por primera vez, las cuatro mejores selecciones del ranking FIFA jugarán las semifinales.

headlines.title: Argentina ya está en semifinales y los expertos también analizan el triunfo de Inglaterra
headlines.description: Nuestros expertos analizaron la clasificación de Argentina a las semifinales de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026 tras vencer a Suiza. Además, repasaron el triunfo de Inglaterra sobre Noruega y las claves que marcaron el encuentro.
